# Competitor Submodules

This directory vendors the main open-source OCR and document parsing competitors used in Leopardi research.

## Direct Competitors

- `paddleocr`: `PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR`
- `hunyuanocr`: `Tencent-Hunyuan/HunyuanOCR`
- `glm-ocr`: `zai-org/GLM-OCR`
- `firered-ocr`: `FireRedTeam/FireRed-OCR`
- `ocrverse`: `DocTron-hub/OCRVerse`
- `deepseek-ocr`: `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-OCR`
- `monkeyocr`: `Yuliang-Liu/MonkeyOCR`
- `dots-ocr`: `rednote-hilab/dots.ocr`
- `olmocr`: `allenai/olmocr`
- `ocrflux`: `chatdoc-com/OCRFlux`
- `openocr`: `Topdu/OpenOCR`
- `mineru`: `opendatalab/MinerU`
- `chandra`: `datalab-to/chandra`
- `inf-mllm`: `infly-ai/INF-MLLM` with `Infinity-Parser`

## Specialist Reference

- `unimernet`: `opendatalab/UniMERNet`

## Local Audit Guidance

Strongest engineering references:

- `olmocr`
- `glm-ocr`
- `paddleocr`
- `openocr`

Strongest product-facing parser toolkits:

- `mineru`
- `monkeyocr`
- `ocrflux`
- `chandra`

Most limited public training openness:

- `hunyuanocr`
- `deepseek-ocr`
- `dots-ocr`
- `firered-ocr`
- `ocrverse`

For the full analysis, see:

- `docs/research/open-source-codebase-audit-2026-04.md`
- `docs/research/leopardi-directions-2026-04.md`
